Package org.efs.bus

package org.efs.bus

EfsEventBus provides a loosely-coupled interface between efs agents. This bus matches publishing agents to subscribing agents using a type+topic key By combining an efs event type with a topic, it allows the same topic to be used with multiple event types. EfsEventBus divides agents into publishers and subscribers. Please note that the same agent may be both a publisher and a subscriber at the same time, even to the same topic key (but why you would want to do that outside of unit testing I cannot imagine).

Note: all agents must be registered with a dispatcher prior to interacting with an event bus. Failure to do so results in the interaction throwing an IllegalStateException.

Publishing Events

An agent announces its ability to publish events on a given topic key by calling EfsEventBus.advertise which returns an EfsEventBus.Advertisement instance. Before publishing any events, the agent must first announce its ability to publish such events by calling Advertisement.publishStatus with a true argument. After setting the publish status to true, the agent is clear to publish events for the given event type topic.

The publishing agent is informed when subscribers join or leave the topic key's feed via its Consumer<EfsSubscribeStatus<E>> callback. This allows the publishing agent to take necessary steps when a new subscribing agent joins or leaves especially when there is the first subscriber to join or the last subscriber to leave.

When a publishing agent is finished publishing on the topic key, it calls Advertisement.close. Once done, the advertisement may not be used again. If the agent needs to start publishing again, it must call EfsEventBus.advertise again and acquire a new Advertisement instance.

Subscribing to Events

An agent subscribes to published events by calling EfsEventBus.subscribe passing in the topic key, publish status callback, event callback, and subscribing agent instance, receiving a EfsEventBus.Subscription instance in return. This Subscription instance is used to close the subscription when the agent no longer wishes to receive events for the given topic.

The subscribing agent is informed of when a new publishing agent advertises itself and when it updates its publish status. This allows an subscriber to know if there are any active publishers for a given topic key. EfsPublishStatus contains a topic key, the number of advertised publishers and the number of active publisher (that is, publishers which have set their publish status to true). If the active publisher count is zero, that means the subscriber will not be receiving any events for the topic key.

Inbox Subscriptions

Suppose an agent is subscribing to a very active topic key and it is possible that events may arrive faster than the agent is able to consume those events. If this happens the agent only wishes to receive the latest event. For example, while the subscribing agent is processing one event another two events arrive. When the current processing is completed, the agent wants to skip the first event and process the second.

In this case, the agent uses EfsEventBus.subscribeInbox. An inbox subscription only forwards the latest event to the subscribing agent. This subscription is most useful when accessing financial market data.

Subscription Router

EfsEventBus.subscribeRouter is used to spread events between multiple agents. An agent may only process a single event at a time. If time-sensitive events arrive at a single agent faster than the agent can process them, then those events delayed on the agent event queue.

A solution to this problem is to create multiple agents for processing those events and create an event router which deals out events among those agents. This distribution may be something simple like round-robin or a more sophisticated weighted distribution based on agent response time or current event queue size.

Wildcard Advertising & Subscribing

This section deals with large topic spaces. Consider an event TopOfBook published on multiple topics with the format /exchange/acme/equity/symbol where symbol is an exchange ticker symbol. This topic format allows an agent to receive market data ticks for a given symbol. Dropping the ticker symbol from the topic would mean the agent would have to receive ticks for each of the exchange's (say) 3,000 symbols. Not a good idea.

But if a publisher or subscriber wishes to interact with multiple ticker feeds (e.g. all symbols starting with A - I), the following shows how this can be done using a single EfsEventBus call rather than hundreds of individual calls.

For publishers:


bus.advertiseAll(TopOfBook.class, "[A-I].*", this::onSubscribeStatus, this::onTopicUpdate, this)

which creates a wildcard advertisement for all TopOfBook topic keys whose topic matches the regular expression "[A-I].*". This wildcard advertisement tracks the concrete topic keys matching the regular expression. The publisher works with these individual advertisements to set publish status and publish events. WildcardAdvertisement.advertisement to access a concrete topic.

For subscribers:


bus.subscribeAll(TopOfBook.class, "[A-I].*", true, this::onPublishStatus, this::onMarketData, this::onTopicUpdate, this)

which creates a org.efs.bus.EfsEventBus.WildcardSubscription<E extends IEfsEvent> instance. This wildcard subscription tracks all the concrete topic keys matching the regular expression.

Closing a wildcard advertisement and subscription closes all the underlying concrete advertisements and subscriptions.

In the above scenario

  • Class
    Description
    EfsEventBus
    TODO: write class description.
    EfsEventBus.Advertisement<E extends IEfsEvent>
    An advertisement is an publishing agent's access point to a event class+topic feed.
    EfsEventBus.Subscription<E extends IEfsEvent>
    Abstract base class for concrete, inbox, and router subscriptions.
    EfsPublishStatus<E extends IEfsEvent>
    Reports a given topic's publish status.
    EfsPublishStatus.Builder<E extends IEfsEvent>
    Builder class used to create EfsPublishStatus instances.
    EfsSubscribeStatus<E extends IEfsEvent>
    This event is sent to publishing agents to inform on number of in-place subscribers for a given topic.
    EfsSubscribeStatus.Builder<E extends IEfsEvent>
    TODO
    IEventRouter<E extends IEfsEvent>
    Implement this interface to dynamically route an event to a given agent and callback pair.
